theme: Xin nghỉ phép & thông báo vắng mặt

vocab.example: I would like to take a day off next Monday.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi muốn xin nghỉ một ngày vào thứ Hai tới.

vocab.example: I need to take sick leave today because of a fever.
vocab.example_vi: Hôm nay tôi cần xin nghỉ ốm vì bị sốt.

vocab.meaning_vi: hỗ trợ thay thế công việc
vocab.example: Can you cover my tasks while I'm on leave?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn hỗ trợ công việc của tôi trong lúc tôi nghỉ được không?

vocab.example: I'm still waiting for approval from my manager.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi vẫn đang đợi sự phê duyệt từ quản lý.

vocab.example: Please request leave at least three days in advance.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng xin nghỉ trước ít nhất ba ngày.

vocab.example: Can we reschedule the meeting to Thursday?
vocab.example_vi: Chúng ta có thể dời cuộc họp sang thứ Năm không?

vocab.example: Please contact Linh if anything urgent comes up.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng liên hệ Linh nếu có việc gì khẩn cấp.

vocab.meaning_vi: vắng mặt tại văn phòng (email tự động)
vocab.example: I will set up an out of office reply before I leave.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi sẽ cài email tự động báo vắng mặt trước khi nghỉ.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Hi Nam, I would like to take a day off next Monday.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Sure, thanks for telling me in advance. Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes, it's a family event. I already sent the request for your approval.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Okay, I will approve it today. Can someone cover your tasks?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes, Minh agreed to cover my tasks for that day.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Great. Please set up an out of office reply too.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I will. If anything urgent comes up, they can contact Minh.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Sounds good. Enjoy your day off.

questions.q: Why does Linh want a day off?
What does Nam remind Linh to set up?

shadowing: I would like to take a day off next Monday for a family event.
Thanks for telling me about your day off so far in advance.
I already sent the leave request to you for your approval.
Can someone cover your tasks while you are away from the office?
Please set up an out of office reply before you leave today.

interview.question: How do you handle your workload when you need to take time off?
tips_vi: Nêu quy trình: báo trước, bàn giao công việc, đặt out of office
Thể hiện tinh thần trách nhiệm với team
Có thể nêu ví dụ cụ thể một lần xin nghỉ
interview.sample_answer: Whenever I need to take time off, I always inform my manager as early as possible, usually a few days in advance, so the team has time to prepare. Before I leave, I make sure to hand over any urgent tasks to a teammate and explain the current status clearly, so there is no confusion while I am away. I also set up an out of office email so people know who to contact if something urgent comes up. Last month, for example, I took two days off and asked a colleague to cover my tasks, and everything went smoothly because we planned it early. I think this kind of planning shows respect for my team and helps the work continue without any problems, even when I am not there. It also makes me feel less stressed during my time off, because I know the team is well prepared.

interview.question: Tell me about a time you had to reschedule something important at work.
tips_vi: Dùng STAR: nêu tình huống cụ thể cần dời lịch
Giải thích cách bạn thông báo và xin lỗi nếu ảnh hưởng người khác
Kết thúc bằng kết quả tích cực
interview.sample_answer: Once I had an important client meeting scheduled for nine in the morning, but I suddenly got a high fever that same morning. I called my manager right away, explained the situation honestly, and asked if we could move the meeting. We agreed to reschedule it to the next day, and I sent a polite email to the client to apologize for the sudden change and explain the reason. The client understood completely, and we even added a short agenda to make the new meeting more efficient. The meeting went well the next day, and the client did not seem upset at all. This experience taught me that clear and quick communication, even about small problems, can solve most scheduling issues before they become bigger ones. Since then, I always try to inform people as early as possible when plans change.
